availability.p: © Duke Databank of Documentary Papyri. This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 License.

div.ab:
Πετεσοῦχος Πατἐν μηνὶ
τοῦ αὐτοῦ ἔτους πυρὸν νέον καθαρὸν
ἄδολον μέτρῳ ᾧ καὶ παρείληφεν
καὶ ἀποκαθεσταμένον εἰς οἶκον
πρὸς Παττοῖς ἰδίοις ἀνη
λώμασι

div.p: The text is a fragmentary grain receipt of a type well attested in Roman Egypt, particularly from the Fayum. The opening names the debtor (Petesouchos son of Pat[---]) and the occasion (a month of the same year — indicating a preceding dating clause now lost). The body stipulates that new, clean, unadulterated wheat must be returned to a storehouse (οἶκος) by the same measure that was originally received, and at the debtor's own transport costs. The formula closely parallels documents such as P.Fay. 86–109, BGU I 12, and the Michigan grain-receipt archive (P.Mich. II 123). Without further context (collection, inventory number, dimensions, associated documents), a precise date and provenance cannot be established.

app.note: Πε[τ]εσοῦχ[ος]: the restoration of tau is certain by spacing and parallels; the patronymic beginning Πατ- is a common Fayumic theophoric name (Πατμῖνις, Πατσᾶυς, Πατσεῦς etc.) — the full form cannot be recovered without further context. ἐν μηνί + month-name is the standard dating formula for this type of grain receipt.

τοῦ αὐτοῦ [ἔτους]: the phrase is formulaic; the regnal year is lost. The restoration πυρὸν νέον καθαρόν follows standard grain-receipt phraseology (cf. P.Fay. 86.3–4; P.Mich. II 123 recto i–vi passim).
ἄδολον [μέτρῳ ᾧ καὶ παρείληφεν]: the phrase ἄδολον μέτρῳ ᾧ καὶ παρείληφεν ("unadulterated, by the same measure as he received") is a fixed formula in private grain receipts of Roman Egypt.
ἀποκαθ[εσταμένον εἰς οἶκον]: "restored/returned to the storehouse"; cf. ἀποκαθίστημι εἰς οἶκον in SB VI 9421.5 and parallels.
τοῖς ἰδίοις ἀνηλώμασι: "at his own expense"; a standard clause specifying that transport costs are borne by the debtor. The line-break between ἀνη- and λώμασι follows the papyrus.

div.p: Petesouchos son of Pat[---] in the month of of the same year: new, pure, [unadulterated] wheat, [by the same measure as he received it], and [restored to the storehouse] to Pat[---], [at his own] expense .
